info.title: Sendmux API
info.description: Programmatic access to your Sendmux email infrastructure.

bearerAuth.description: Sendmux API key. Use a root API key for Management API routes, or a mailbox credential for Mailbox API routes. Obtain keys from the dashboard under API Keys.

field.description: Dot-path of the offending request field.

code.description: Machine-readable issue code from the validator (e.g. zod issue code).

param.description: The parameter that caused the error

retryable.description: Whether the caller may safely retry this request. 4xx are typically false (except 429); 5xx are typically true.

errors.description: Accumulated per-field issues for validation errors. Only present on 400/422 responses.

amazon_ses.description: Number of shared Amazon SES accounts

type.description: Sending account type. `amazon_ses` is the shared managed account.

status_reason.description: Public-safe reason for the current status.

is_active.description: Whether this account is enabled for sending.

is_shared.description: True for the team's shared Amazon SES account.

is_editable.description: False when the account is platform-managed.

smtp_host.description: SMTP host for custom SMTP accounts.

smtp_port.description: SMTP port for custom SMTP accounts.

smtp_username.description: SMTP username for custom SMTP accounts.

has_smtp_password.description: Whether a custom SMTP password is stored.

oauth_email.description: Connected account email for OAuth accounts.

has_refresh_token.description: Whether an OAuth account has an active connection token.

per_second.description: Quota value. Use a number for a fixed cap, a min/max range, or null to clear on PATCH.

per_minute.description: Quota value. Use a number for a fixed cap, a min/max range, or null to clear on PATCH.

per_hour.description: Quota value. Use a number for a fixed cap, a min/max range, or null to clear on PATCH.

per_day.description: Quota value. Use a number for a fixed cap, a min/max range, or null to clear on PATCH.

per_second.description: Quota value. Use a number for a fixed cap, a min/max range, or null to clear on PATCH.

per_minute.description: Quota value. Use a number for a fixed cap, a min/max range, or null to clear on PATCH.

per_hour.description: Quota value. Use a number for a fixed cap, a min/max range, or null to clear on PATCH.

per_day.description: Quota value. Use a number for a fixed cap, a min/max range, or null to clear on PATCH.

sent_from_email.description: The actual from address used for delivery after transformation. NULL if no transformation occurred or for older log entries.

sent_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when email was sent

MailboxDomainNameValueRecord.description: SPF TXT record covering both SES and Sendmux includes

mx.description: MX records the customer must place. All point at Sendmux's inbound mail servers.

verified_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of last successful verification

mailbox_count.description: Active mailboxes currently using this domain

mx.description: MX record present with priority 10 pointing at mail.sendmux.ai

verification_txt.description: Ownership TXT record present with correct value

spf.description: SPF TXT record matches expected value

dmarc.description: DMARC TXT record matches expected value

ses_dkim_status.description: Latest SES DKIM status from AWS

type.description: Outbound routing strategy. `all` lets the mailbox use any team-configured provider; `providers` restricts to the listed provider IDs; `group` routes via a delivery group.

group_public_ids.description: Delivery group public IDs (only when type=group)

display_name.description: Optional display name shown in outbound From headers

quota_bytes.description: Storage quota in bytes. Current writable tiers are 1 GB, 5 GB, and 50 GB.

secret.description: Mailbox credential — shown exactly once. Use as a Bearer token on the HTTP API and as the password for IMAP retrieval and SMTP submission.

username.description: Mailbox email address used as the login

MailboxCredential.description: Initial credential for the mailbox. `null` if credential generation failed — call POST /mailboxes/{id}/keys to retry.

warning.description: Optional warning string when the mailbox was created without an initial credential

quota_bytes.description: Storage quota in bytes. Allowed values: 1073741824 (1 GB), 5368709120 (5 GB), 53687091200 (50 GB).

quota_used_bytes.description: Live storage usage in bytes. Omitted when usage is temporarily unavailable.

gated.description: Flags are true when a feature is intentionally unavailable through the mailbox API.

may_delete.description: Whether the mailbox service allows this identity to be deleted.

name.description: Default sender name. Send null or an empty string to clear it.

text_signature.description: Plain text signature appended to Mailbox API sends that include text_body.

html_signature.description: HTML signature appended to Mailbox API sends that include html_body.

keywords.description: Active message keywords, including system flags and custom labels.

html.description: HTML body when requested. Returned as a JSON string and not as rendered content.

attachments.description: Attachment metadata only. Attachment contents are not parsed by this endpoint.

keywords.description: Map of message keyword names to booleans. `true` sets the keyword; `false` clears it. Keyword names are normalised to lowercase. Lifecycle keywords such as `$draft` are read-only.

if_in_state.description: Optional message state token for stale-write protection.

permanent.description: When true, permanently deletes instead of moving to Trash.

if_in_state.description: Optional message state token for stale-write protection.

subject.description: Subject snippet. May include mark tags.

preview.description: Body preview snippet. May include mark tags.

blob_id.description: Uploaded attachment blob ID for a later send request.

content.description: Base64-encoded content for small inline attachments. Use /mailbox/attachments:upload for larger files.

keywords.description: Map of message keyword names to booleans. `true` sets the keyword; `false` clears it. Keyword names are normalised to lowercase. Lifecycle keywords such as `$draft` are read-only.

role.description: System role when the folder is a built-in folder.

can_add_items.description: Whether messages can be moved into this folder.

pattern.description: Email address or bare domain name. Validated against RFC 5321/1035 regexes.

note.description: Free-form caption for the rule (≤255 chars). UI-only metadata — never consulted at match time. `null` when absent.

mode.description: Active filtering mode. `off` disables filtering (all senders accepted). `allowlist` only accepts senders matching a rule. `denylist` blocks senders matching a rule.

rules.description: Current rules, sorted by pattern ascending
Replacement rule set. Maximum 1000 rules per request. An empty array is valid (no rules).

amount.description: Billable amount for this message in USD

mailbox_ids.description: Mailbox public IDs to receive events for. Empty means all mailboxes in the team.

WebhookFilters.description: Optional delivery scope. Empty mailbox_ids means all team mailboxes.

name.description: Optional human-friendly label used in dashboard list/detail pages. May be `null` for subscriptions created without a name.

url.description: HTTPS endpoint that receives signed event POSTs.

items.description: Event types a webhook may subscribe to. `sendmux.test` is accepted so you can verify end-to-end delivery via POST /webhooks/{id}/test.

enabled.description: When false, no events are delivered to this subscription.

failing.description: True when the 24-hour retry window has been exhausted for this subscription. Reset by updating the subscription (e.g. fixing the URL) and re-enabling.

secret.description: Signing secret used to verify the HMAC-SHA256 signature on every event POST. This is the ONLY response containing the raw secret — store it securely; it cannot be retrieved later. Use POST /webhooks/{id}/rotate-secret to issue a new one.

name.description: Optional human-friendly label (max 255 chars). Surfaced in dashboard list/detail views.

url.description: HTTPS endpoint that will receive event POSTs. Must be https:// — plain HTTP is rejected.

items.description: Event types a webhook may subscribe to. `sendmux.test` is accepted so you can verify end-to-end delivery via POST /webhooks/{id}/test.

event_types.description: At least one event type must be provided.

allOf.description: Optional delivery scope. Omit or pass an empty mailbox_ids array to receive matching events for all mailboxes in the team.

name.description: Replace the human-friendly label. Send `null` to clear it; omit to leave unchanged.

url.description: New HTTPS endpoint. Must be https://.

items.description: Event types a webhook may subscribe to. `sendmux.test` is accepted so you can verify end-to-end delivery via POST /webhooks/{id}/test.

event_types.description: Replaces the full event-type list. At least one must be provided.

allOf.description: Replaces the full delivery scope. Omit to leave unchanged; pass an empty mailbox_ids array for all team mailboxes.

enabled.description: When toggled from false to true, also clears the `failing` flag — retry deliveries resume on the next matching event.

event_type.description: Event types a webhook may subscribe to. `sendmux.test` is accepted so you can verify end-to-end delivery via POST /webhooks/{id}/test.

response_status.description: HTTP status returned by the endpoint, or null for network/timeout failures.

max_attempts.description: Maximum attempts before the subscription is marked failing.

next_retry_at.description: Next retry timestamp when result is retrying; otherwise null.

error_message.description: Public-safe network error category when no HTTP response was received.

response_body.description: Truncated endpoint response preview, if any.

payload_available.description: True while the delivered request payload can still be fetched.

payload_expires_at.description: Timestamp when the retained request payload expires.

request_payload_bytes.description: Size of the delivered request payload in bytes.

request_payload_sha256.description: SHA-256 digest of the delivered request payload.

message_id.description: Message identifier from the event source.

message_id_kind.description: `provider` for sending feedback IDs; `rfc5322` for inbound email Message-ID header values.

sendmux_message_id.description: Sendmux message correlation ID for outbound sending feedback when available.

recipients.description: All affected recipients. Empty for synthetic test events.

mailbox_id.description: Mailbox public ID for inbound mailbox events when resolved.

bounce_subtype.description: More specific bounce category when available.

complaint_subtype.description: More specific complaint category when available.

delay_expires_at.description: When delivery retries are expected to stop, when provided.

is_spam.description: Only populated for inbound mailbox events.

webhook_id.description: Target webhook ID for synthetic `sendmux.test` payloads.

id.description: Stable per-event identifier. Also sent in the X-Sendmux-Event-Id header.

type.description: Dotted event type. Same value as the X-Sendmux-Event-Type header.

WebhookEventPayload.description: The JSON request body delivered to the webhook endpoint.

event_type.description: Event types a webhook may subscribe to. `sendmux.test` is accepted so you can verify end-to-end delivery via POST /webhooks/{id}/test.

expires_at.description: Timestamp when this retained payload expires.

event_id.description: The event_id attached to the X-Sendmux-Event-Id header of the test delivery.

get.description: Returns aggregate counts of configured sending accounts with breakdowns by type and status.
Returns a cursor-paginated list of sending accounts. Credentials are never returned. The shared Amazon SES account is included and marked with `is_shared: true`.

schema.description: Pagination cursor — the `next_cursor` from the previous response.

post.description: Creates a custom SMTP sending account. Supply an `Idempotency-Key` header to safely retry on network errors. The SMTP password is stored securely and is never returned.

schema.description: Client-chosen unique key to safely retry the request. Cached for 24h per (team, endpoint, key). Different body with same key returns 409 idempotency_conflict.

Location.description: Canonical GET URL for the new sending account.

409.description: Limit reached or idempotency conflict

get.description: Returns one sending account. Responses include an ETag for conditional GET and optimistic PATCH.

schema.description: Weak ETag from a previous response. Returns 304 when unchanged.

patch.description: Updates a custom sending account. The shared Amazon SES account cannot be edited here; use activate/deactivate and the limit-request endpoint for the allowed shared-account actions.

schema.description: Weak ETag from a prior GET. Reject with 409 conflict when the server's current ETag differs.

409.description: ETag conflict

delete.description: Deletes a custom or connected sending account. The shared Amazon SES account cannot be deleted.

post.description: Enables a sending account. OAuth accounts without an active connection are moved to pending until reconnected in the dashboard. The shared Amazon SES account can be activated.

schema.description: Client-chosen unique key to safely retry the request. Cached for 24h per (team, endpoint, key). Different body with same key returns 409 idempotency_conflict.

409.description: Idempotency conflict

post.description: Disables a sending account. The shared Amazon SES account can be deactivated.

schema.description: Client-chosen unique key to safely retry the request. Cached for 24h per (team, endpoint, key). Different body with same key returns 409 idempotency_conflict.

409.description: Idempotency conflict

post.description: Runs a connection test for a custom SMTP account. The shared Amazon SES account and OAuth accounts cannot be tested through this endpoint.

schema.description: Client-chosen unique key to safely retry the request. Cached for 24h per (team, endpoint, key). Different body with same key returns 409 idempotency_conflict.

get.description: Returns per-account delivery-log counts for today and the trailing 7-day window.
Returns custom-account capacity and shared Amazon SES daily sending limit state.

post.summary: Request a sending account limit increase
post.description: Creates a request to increase the number of custom or connected sending accounts allowed for the team.

schema.description: Client-chosen unique key to safely retry the request. Cached for 24h per (team, endpoint, key). Different body with same key returns 409 idempotency_conflict.

409.description: A request is already pending

422.description: Increase request is not available yet

get.summary: Get shared Amazon SES limit request state
get.description: Returns the shared Amazon SES daily limit state and any pending increase request.

200.description: Shared Amazon SES limit request state

post.summary: Request a shared Amazon SES daily limit increase
post.description: Creates a review request for the shared Amazon SES daily sending limit. This does not modify the limit directly.

schema.description: Client-chosen unique key to safely retry the request. Cached for 24h per (team, endpoint, key). Different body with same key returns 409 idempotency_conflict.

404.description: Shared Amazon SES account not found

409.description: A request is already pending

422.description: Increase request is not available yet

get.description: Returns aggregated email delivery counts with flexible time windowing and comparison period.
Returns paginated delivery logs with cursor-based pagination and filters.

schema.description: Search to_email, from_email, sent_from_email, subject, message_id

get.description: Returns a single delivery log by its public ID. Responses carry a weak `ETag` header — send it back as `If-None-Match` on the next request and the server will return `304 Not Modified` (no body) when the log has not changed. Logs in a terminal status (sent/failed/rejected) stop changing, so 304 replay is a real bandwidth win on repeated polls.

schema.description: Weak ETag from a previous response. Returns 304 when unchanged.

304.description: Not Modified — the client's cached copy (matching `If-None-Match`) is still current.

get.description: Returns the current team balance and auto top-up configuration.
Returns a spend summary over a configurable lookback period.

Returns paginated credit transaction history with optional type filter. Uses cursor-based pagination ordered by created_at descending — pass `cursor=<next_cursor>` from the previous response to fetch the next page.

schema.description: Pagination cursor — the `next_cursor` from the previous response.

get.description: Returns a cursor-paginated list of sending domains configured for the team, ordered by `created_at` descending. Each entry includes the DNS records the customer must place, the current verification status, and the SES DKIM state. Pass `cursor=<next_cursor>` from the previous response to fetch the next page.

schema.description: Pagination cursor — the `next_cursor` from the previous response.

post.description:
Creates a new sending domain and returns the DNS records the customer must place before verification can succeed. Provisioning touches both our mail platform and Amazon SES — on any failure the partial state is automatically rolled back.

Supply an `Idempotency-Key` header (any unique string, max 255 chars) to safely retry on network errors. Replays with the same key return the original response; replays with a different body return `409 idempotency_conflict`.

schema.description: Client-chosen unique key to safely retry the request. Cached for 24h per (team, endpoint, key). Different body with same key returns 409 idempotency_conflict.

domain.description: Fully qualified domain name. Must be lowercased and domain-only (no scheme).

201.description: Domain created. The response carries a `Location` header pointing at the canonical GET URL for the new domain.

Location.description: Canonical GET URL for the newly created domain.

409.description: Domain already in use, or `Idempotency-Key` was replayed with a different body.

503.description: SES tenant not yet provisioned for this team

get.description: Returns a single domain including its DNS records and latest verification state. Responses carry a weak `ETag` header — send it back as `If-None-Match` on the next request and the server will return `304 Not Modified` (no body) when the resource has not changed.

schema.description: Weak ETag from a previous response. Returns 304 when unchanged.

304.description: Not Modified — the client's cached copy (matching `If-None-Match`) is still current.

delete.description: Removes a domain from our mail platform, Amazon SES, and Sendmux. Blocked if any active mailbox still uses the domain — delete those mailboxes first.

409.description: Domain still has active mailboxes (error code `conflict`).

get.summary: Download a domain as a zone file
get.description: Returns the domain's DNS records formatted as a BIND-style zone file fragment. Suitable for direct paste into a DNS provider that accepts zone-file imports.

post.description: Runs an immediate DNS-over-HTTPS check of the domain's MX, SPF, DMARC and ownership TXT records, then asks SES for the latest DKIM status. If every check passes the domain is marked verified. Domains automatically re-verify every 6 hours — this endpoint is only needed to trigger a check on demand.

get.description: Returns a cursor-paginated list of mailboxes configured for the team, ordered by `created_at` descending. Pass `cursor=<next_cursor>` from the previous response to fetch the next page.

schema.description: Pagination cursor — the `next_cursor` from the previous response.
Set to `true` to include soft-deleted mailboxes (status `deleted`) in the response. Default `false`. Soft-deleted mailboxes are retained for tenant-isolation references in delivery logs and API keys.

post.description:
Provisions a new mailbox plus an initial bearer token and matching IMAP/SMTP mailbox password. The domain portion of the email must already be verified via POST /domains. If supplied, `quota_bytes` must be one of the current storage tiers: 1073741824 (1 GB), 5368709120 (5 GB), or 53687091200 (50 GB). Creation provisions resources on our mail platform and, for `@myagent.mx` addresses, on Amazon SES — any failure rolls the partial state back automatically.

Supply an `Idempotency-Key` header (any unique string, max 255 chars) to safely retry on network errors. Replays with the same key return the original response; replays with a different body return `409 idempotency_conflict`.

schema.description: Client-chosen unique key to safely retry the request. Cached for 24h per (team, endpoint, key). Different body with same key returns 409 idempotency_conflict.

quota_bytes.description: Storage quota in bytes. Allowed values: 1073741824 (1 GB), 5368709120 (5 GB), 53687091200 (50 GB).

201.description: Mailbox created. The response carries a `Location` header pointing at the canonical GET URL for the new mailbox.

Location.description: Canonical GET URL for the newly created mailbox.

409.description: Email already in use (error code `conflict`), or `Idempotency-Key` was replayed with a different body (`idempotency_conflict`).

422.description: Domain not yet verified or semantic validation failure

503.description: Team is not yet provisioned for sending

get.description: Returns a single mailbox, tenant-scoped to the caller's team. Responses carry a weak `ETag` header — send it back as `If-None-Match` on the next request and the server will return `304 Not Modified` (no body) when the resource has not changed. The same ETag is the value to use in `If-Match` on PATCH for optimistic concurrency.

schema.description: Weak ETag from a previous response. Returns 304 when unchanged.

ETag.description: Weak ETag for conditional GETs and optimistic-concurrency PATCHes.

304.description: Not Modified — the client's cached copy (matching `If-None-Match`) is still current.

patch.description:
Updates any combination of display_name, quota_bytes, and send_scope on a mailbox. The display_name and send_scope changes land in a single transaction; quota_bytes is applied afterwards because it also updates external state on our mail platform. Quota can only be set to 1073741824 (1 GB), 5368709120 (5 GB), or 53687091200 (50 GB), and cannot be shrunk below the mailbox's current usage.

For optimistic concurrency, send `If-Match: <etag>` using the ETag from a prior GET. A mismatched `If-Match` returns `409 conflict` (the server's ETag is echoed back so you can decide whether to re-fetch). Responses carry the new ETag so chained edits can pipeline without a re-GET.

schema.description: Weak ETag from a prior GET. Reject with 409 conflict when the server's current ETag differs.

ETag.description: Weak ETag for the post-update resource.

409.description: `If-Match` ETag does not match the server's current version.

422.description: Validation error — unsupported quota tier, quota below current usage, or cross-tenant send_scope reference

delete.description: Removes a mailbox from our mail platform, Amazon SES (for `@myagent.mx` addresses), and Sendmux. All associated API keys are revoked.

post.description: Suspends a mailbox without deleting messages, credentials, or settings. Suspended mailboxes cannot receive mail, authenticate to mailbox protocols, or send from the mailbox address. The operation is idempotent and accepts `Idempotency-Key` for safe retries.

schema.description: Client-chosen unique key to safely retry the request. Cached for 24h per (team, endpoint, key).

409.description: Mailbox is deleted, or `Idempotency-Key` was replayed with a different request body.

post.description: Restores a suspended mailbox so it can authenticate, receive mail, and send from the mailbox address again. The operation is idempotent and accepts `Idempotency-Key` for safe retries.

schema.description: Client-chosen unique key to safely retry the request. Cached for 24h per (team, endpoint, key).

409.description: Mailbox is deleted, or `Idempotency-Key` was replayed with a different request body.

post.description:
Mints an additional bearer token plus IMAP/SMTP mailbox password for an existing mailbox. The plaintext key and password are shown exactly once — store them immediately.

Supply an `Idempotency-Key` header (any unique string, max 255 chars) to safely retry on network errors. Replays with the same key return the original response; replays with a different body return `409 idempotency_conflict`.

schema.description: Client-chosen unique key to safely retry the request. Cached for 24h per (team, endpoint, key). Different body with same key returns 409 idempotency_conflict.

201.description: Mailbox API key created. The response carries a `Location` header pointing at the canonical URL for the new key.

Location.description: Canonical URL for the newly created API key.

409.description: API key limit reached (`limit_exceeded`) or idempotency key conflict (`idempotency_conflict`).

delete.description: Revokes a specific bearer token + matching mailbox password. Idempotent on already-revoked keys.

get.description:
Returns the mailbox the bearer token is scoped to, including live storage usage. Intended for SDK auto-discovery — call once on startup to resolve the mailbox ID. Requires a mailbox-scoped API key; root keys receive 403.

Responses carry a weak `ETag` header — send it back as `If-None-Match` on the next request and the server will return `304 Not Modified` (no body) when the mailbox state has not changed.

schema.description: Weak ETag from a previous response. Returns 304 when unchanged.

304.description: Not Modified — the client's cached copy (matching `If-None-Match`) is still current.

403.description: Root key used on a mailbox-scoped endpoint

get.description: Returns mailbox API capabilities, resource state tokens, limits, and disabled feature flags for the authenticated mailbox.
Returns the default sender identity and signatures for the authenticated mailbox.

patch.description: Updates the default sender name, plain text signature, and HTML signature for the authenticated mailbox.

get.description: Returns sender identities available to the authenticated mailbox.
Returns a cursor-paginated list of send submissions for the authenticated mailbox, including delivery lifecycle fields when available.
Returns send submission changes since a prior submission state, or the current state when omitted.
Returns one send submission for the authenticated mailbox. Responses include a weak `ETag` header.
Returns a cursor-paginated list of quota usage and limits for the authenticated mailbox.
Returns quota changes since a prior quota state, or the current quota state when omitted.
Returns a compact usage view derived from mailbox quota records. Responses include a weak `ETag` header.
Returns a cursor-paginated list of messages for the authenticated mailbox. Requires a mailbox API key.
Returns filtered message-list index changes since a prior message query state, or the current message query state when omitted.

400.description: Invalid query sync state or filter

post.description: Returns exact messages by ID. Use `body_mode` to choose summary-only output, raw body output, or clean JSON content. Attachment output remains metadata only; attachment contents are not parsed.
Updates allowed message flags and keywords for up to 100 messages with state-safe conflict handling.

409.description: State conflict

post.description: Moves up to 100 messages to Trash by default with state-safe conflict handling. Set `permanent=true` to permanently delete them.

409.description: State conflict

get.description: Returns a count for the supplied mailbox message filters without returning message rows.
Returns subject and preview snippets for a text search. Provide `message_ids` to snippet exact messages, or omit it to snippet the first filtered results.

post.description: Uploads one attachment as binary data and returns a blob ID that can be supplied to `POST /mailbox/messages/send`.

schema.description: Filename to use when sending the uploaded attachment.

post.description: Creates and queues a message from the authenticated mailbox. Supply an `Idempotency-Key` header to safely retry. Attachments may use small inline base64 content or blob IDs returned by `POST /mailbox/attachments:upload`.

409.description: Idempotency conflict

get.description: Returns one message from the authenticated mailbox. Responses include a weak `ETag` header.

patch.description: Updates mutable message flags and keywords with state-safe conflict handling. Send `If-Match` with a prior ETag to reject stale edits.

409.description: ETag conflict

delete.description: Moves a message to Trash by default with state-safe conflict handling. Set `permanent=true` to permanently delete it. Send `If-Match` with a prior ETag to reject stale deletes.

409.description: ETag or state conflict

get.description: Returns raw message body content for the authenticated mailbox. This endpoint does not strip signatures, strip quoted replies, extract links, or clean the body into a structured content shape.
Returns deterministic clean JSON content for one message. Attachment output is metadata only; attachment contents are not parsed by this endpoint.
Returns a cursor-paginated list of conversation threads for the authenticated mailbox. Filter by text, participant, folder, unread state, attachment presence, or date range.
Returns thread metadata, message IDs, participants, last message, unread count, and state for one conversation. Responses include a weak `ETag` header.
Returns cursor-paginated message summaries for one thread. Use the thread content endpoint when you need cleaned bodies.
Returns a cursor-paginated clean JSON view of messages in a thread. Attachment output is metadata only; attachment contents are not parsed by this endpoint.
Streams one attachment from a message in the authenticated mailbox. Supports standard byte ranges.
Returns a cursor-paginated list of folders for the authenticated mailbox.

post.description: Creates a folder in the authenticated mailbox.

get.description: Returns folder changes since a prior folder state, or the current folder state when omitted.
Returns folder-list index changes since a prior folder query state, or the current folder query state when omitted.
Returns one folder from the authenticated mailbox. Responses include a weak `ETag` header.

patch.description: Updates a folder with state-safe conflict handling. Send `If-Match` with a prior ETag to reject stale edits.

409.description: ETag conflict

delete.description: Deletes an empty custom folder with state-safe conflict handling. Built-in folders and non-empty folders cannot be deleted. Send `If-Match` with a prior ETag to reject stale deletes.

409.description: Folder cannot be deleted

get.description: Returns message changes in the legacy shape when `types` is omitted. Pass a comma-separated `types` list for a typed state map.

schema.description: Comma-separated list: messages, folders, threads, submissions, identities, quotas.

get.description: Streams bounded rich mailbox events for connected clients. Each received-message event includes subject, participants, preview, attachment metadata, and a capped body snapshot; use the message endpoints for full content or attachment bytes.

200.description: Server-sent event stream. Event data uses the MailboxRealtimeEvent shape.

get.description:
Returns the current sender-filter mode and rule set for a mailbox. Mailbox-scoped rules override any domain-wide rules set on the parent domain.

Responses carry a weak `ETag` — send it as `If-None-Match` to skip the body when the filter set has not changed (returns `304 Not Modified`). The same ETag is the value to use in `If-Match` on the corresponding PUT for optimistic concurrency.

schema.description: Weak ETag from a previous response. Returns 304 when unchanged.

ETag.description: Weak ETag for conditional GETs and optimistic-concurrency PUTs.

304.description: Not Modified — the client's cached copy (matching `If-None-Match`) is still current.

put.description:
Atomically replaces the sender-filter mode and rule set for a mailbox. The entire rule set is swapped in a single operation — there is no intermediate state where the mailbox has a partial ruleset. A maximum of 1000 rules per request is enforced.

For optimistic concurrency, send `If-Match: <etag>` using the ETag from a prior GET. A mismatched `If-Match` returns `409 conflict` (the server's ETag is echoed back so you can decide whether to re-fetch). Responses carry the new ETag so chained edits can pipeline without a re-GET.

schema.description: Weak ETag from a prior GET. Reject with 409 conflict when the server's current ETag differs.

ETag.description: Weak ETag for the post-update resource.

409.description: `If-Match` ETag does not match the server's current version.

422.description: Rule cap exceeded or invalid rule pattern

get.description:
Returns the current sender-filter mode and rule set applied to every mailbox under this domain. Per-mailbox rules take precedence at match time.

Responses carry a weak `ETag` — send it as `If-None-Match` to skip the body when the filter set has not changed (returns `304 Not Modified`). The same ETag is the value to use in `If-Match` on the corresponding PUT for optimistic concurrency.

schema.description: Weak ETag from a previous response. Returns 304 when unchanged.

ETag.description: Weak ETag for conditional GETs and optimistic-concurrency PUTs.

304.description: Not Modified — the client's cached copy (matching `If-None-Match`) is still current.

put.description:
Atomically replaces the sender-filter mode and rule set for an entire domain. Applies to every mailbox under the domain unless that mailbox has its own per-mailbox rules (which take precedence). Maximum 1000 rules per request.

For optimistic concurrency, send `If-Match: <etag>` using the ETag from a prior GET. A mismatched `If-Match` returns `409 conflict` (the server's ETag is echoed back so you can decide whether to re-fetch). Responses carry the new ETag so chained edits can pipeline without a re-GET.

schema.description: Weak ETag from a prior GET. Reject with 409 conflict when the server's current ETag differs.

ETag.description: Weak ETag for the post-update resource.

409.description: `If-Match` ETag does not match the server's current version.

422.description: Rule cap exceeded or invalid rule pattern

get.description: Returns paginated incoming message logs for billing accountability.

schema.description: Search mailbox, sender, recipient, subject, or message ID

get.description: Returns one incoming message log by public ID.

Returns a cursor-paginated list of webhook subscriptions configured for the team, ordered by `created_at` descending. The signing secret is never included. Pass `cursor=<next_cursor>` from the previous response to fetch the next page.

schema.description: Pagination cursor — the `next_cursor` from the previous response.

post.description:
Creates a new webhook subscription and returns the signing secret ONCE in the response body. Store it securely — it cannot be retrieved later. Use `POST /webhooks/{id}/rotate-secret` to issue a new one.

Supply an `Idempotency-Key` header (any unique string, max 255 chars) to safely retry on network errors. Replays with the same key return the original response; replays with a different body return `409 idempotency_conflict`.

schema.description: Client-chosen unique key to safely retry the request. Cached for 24h per (team, endpoint, key). Different body with same key returns 409 idempotency_conflict.

201.description: Subscription created, including the signing secret. The response carries a `Location` header pointing at the canonical GET URL for the new subscription.

Location.description: Canonical GET URL for the newly created webhook subscription.

409.description: `Idempotency-Key` was replayed with a different body (`idempotency_conflict`).

422.description: Validation failed (e.g. non-https URL, unknown event type)

get.description: Returns a single webhook subscription. The signing secret is never included. Responses carry a weak `ETag` header — send it back as `If-None-Match` on the next request and the server will return `304 Not Modified` (no body) when the resource has not changed. The same ETag is the value to use in `If-Match` on PATCH for optimistic concurrency.

schema.description: Weak ETag from a previous response. Returns 304 when unchanged.

ETag.description: Weak ETag for conditional GETs and optimistic-concurrency PATCHes.

304.description: Not Modified — the client's cached copy (matching `If-None-Match`) is still current.

patch.description:
Updates the URL, event types, or enabled flag. Any field omitted from the request body is left unchanged. The signing secret is unaffected — use `POST /webhooks/{id}/rotate-secret` to rotate it.

For optimistic concurrency, send `If-Match: <etag>` using the ETag from a prior GET. A mismatched `If-Match` returns `409 conflict` (the server's ETag is echoed back so you can decide whether to re-fetch). Responses carry the new ETag so chained edits can pipeline without a re-GET.

schema.description: Weak ETag from a prior GET. Reject with 409 conflict when the server's current ETag differs.

ETag.description: Weak ETag for the post-update resource.

409.description: `If-Match` ETag does not match the server's current version.

422.description: Validation failed (e.g. non-https URL, unknown event type, empty event list)

delete.description: Permanently removes the subscription. In-flight retries are dropped.

get.description: Returns recent delivery attempts for one webhook subscription, ordered by `created_at` descending. Delivery metadata is retained for 7 days. Use the payload endpoint while `payload_available` is true to inspect the exact JSON request body that was delivered.

schema.description: Pagination cursor — the `next_cursor` from the previous response.

get.description: Returns the retained JSON request body for one delivery attempt. Payloads are retained for 7 days and may no longer be available after `payload_expires_at`.

404.description: Delivery not found or payload no longer available

post.description:
Generates a fresh signing secret and returns it ONCE. The old secret is immediately invalidated — the next event delivery is signed with the new secret. Store the new value securely; it cannot be retrieved later.

Supply an `Idempotency-Key` header to safely retry on network errors — replays under the same key return the original rotation response rather than minting a fresh secret. Replays against a different `public_id` under the same key return `409 idempotency_conflict`.

schema.description: Client-chosen unique key to safely retry the request. Cached for 24h per (team, endpoint, key). Different target `public_id` under the same key returns 409 idempotency_conflict.

409.description: `Idempotency-Key` was replayed against a different webhook (`idempotency_conflict`).

post.description:
Publishes a synthetic `sendmux.test` event to this webhook subscription. The subscription must include `"sendmux.test"` in `event_types`, and mailbox filters do not block the test delivery. Returns the generated `event_id` so you can correlate the `X-Sendmux-Event-Id` header on your endpoint.

Supply an `Idempotency-Key` header to safely retry on network errors — replays under the same key return the original `event_id` rather than publishing a second test event.

schema.description: Client-chosen unique key to safely retry the request. Cached for 24h per (team, endpoint, key). Different target `public_id` under the same key returns 409 idempotency_conflict.

409.description: `Idempotency-Key` was replayed against a different webhook (`idempotency_conflict`) or the webhook is not subscribed to `sendmux.test` (`conflict`).
